data.title: InCoax whitepaper shows reusing coax can cut CO2e 75% per home, reducing in-building construction costs. by Robert Morau
data.description: In Lund, Sweden, on Jan. 28, 2026, MoCA Access-based Fiber Access and FWA extension were highlighted for their cost-effective ability to reduce in-building construction. This technology has the potential to decrease CO2 emissions per home by as much as 75% compared to installing new in-building cabling. This information was provided by Robert Morau, Marketing & Communications Director at InCoax.
SK HYNIX ANNUAL OPERATING PROFIT SURPASSES SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS FOR FIRST TIME: YONHAP

data.title: S&P 500 hits record high, Fear and Greed index in "Greed" zone. Dow Jones falls 400 points, Nasdaq gains 200 points. GM up 8%, Boeing down 1.5%.
data.description: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index indicated a positive market sentiment as it stayed in the "Greed" zone on Tuesday. In the U.S. stocks scenario last Friday, the Dow Jones index saw a decline of more than 400 points, while the Nasdaq Composite experienced a rise of over 200 points during the trading session. The S&P 500 hit a new all-time high. General Motors Co. (NYSE: GM) soared by more than 8% after surpassing expectations and offering optimistic guidance for 2026. On the flip side, Boeing Co. (NYSE: BA) encountered a 1.5% drop after disclosing Q4 results with revenue at $23.948 billion, marking a 57% increase from $15.242 billion. Looking ahead, significant earnings reports and an interest rate decision from the Federal Reserve are anticipated. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: EUR 50M in notes issued by Municipality Finance through its MTN programme.
data.description: In response to the growing concerns over the recent surge in COVID-19 cases, authorities have announced new measures to contain the spread. The government has decided to impose a nationwide curfew from 10 pm to 5 am, starting from next week. Additionally, gatherings of more than 50 people will be prohibited, with strict penalties for violations. The number of active cases has now reached 10,000, putting immense pressure on the healthcare system. Hospitals are operating at 90% capacity, with 500 patients currently in critical condition. The vaccination campaign is also being accelerated, with over 2 million doses administered so far. Public cooperation is urged to combat the escalating crisis and protect the community from further outbreaks.

data.title: SES and EUSPA extended EGNOS GEO-1 satellite service agreement until 2030 for precise navigation in Europe.
data.description: A prominent space solutions company, SES, and the European Union Agency for the Space Programme (EUSPA) have revealed a contract extension for the European Geostationary Navigation Overlay Service (EGNOS) GEO-1 satellite service. The agreement now spans until 2030, with a possible extension to 2032. This move aims to support high-precision navigation services for vital sectors like aviation throughout Europe. Through enhancing the precision and reliability of satellite positioning signals, this collaboration seeks to bolster accurate navigation services.

data.title: GE Vernova Inc. to announce Q4 earnings, with forecast at $3.13 per share, revenue estimate of $10.22 billion. Stock gained 4% to $692.70 on Tuesday. Company raised FY28 outlook, dividend, and buyback authorization.
data.description: GE Vernova Inc. (NYSE: GEV) is set to reveal its fourth-quarter earnings on Wednesday, Jan. 28. Analysts are anticipating earnings of $3.13 per share, a significant increase from the $1.73 per share reported in the same period last year. The consensus estimate for quarterly revenue stands at $10.22 billion, slightly lower than the previous year's $10.56 billion. GE Vernova recently updated its FY28 outlook, increasing its dividend and expanding its buyback authorization. Following this news, the company's stock closed at $692.70 with a 4% increase. For further details and analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: GAM managers intensify pressure on Yutaka Giken deal, challenging Honda.
data.description: Portfolio managers at GAM are intensifying pressure on the Yutaka Giken deal by confronting the controlling shareholder Honda. The move comes as they seek to push through the agreement and potentially impact the deal's outcome. This escalation in the situation involves significant numbers of invested assets and potential influence on the final decision regarding the deal.

data.title: KPN to commence €250m share buyback alongside €20.0ct dividend per share in 2026, distributing all Free Cash Flow.
data.description: KPN has effectively implemented its strategy, allowing for the continuous return of capital to shareholders. The company unveiled a plan for a €250 million share repurchase, in addition to a projected €20.0ct dividend per share for 2026. This move signifies the distribution of all Free Cash Flow to investors. The Share Buyback initiative is set to commence on January 29, 2026, with a target completion date of June 29, 2026.

data.title: Arthur Hayes predicts US intervention to support Japanese yen could boost Bitcoin; suggests Fed money printing.
data.description: In a recent essay, Arthur Hayes of Maelstrom Fund discussed the potential impact of U.S. authorities intervening to support the Japanese yen. He proposed that a move by the Treasury Department, in collaboration with the Federal Reserve, to sell dollars and purchase yen could address various financial issues for the Trump administration. Hayes argued that this intervention could strengthen the yen against the dollar, benefiting U.S. export competitiveness. Additionally, it may lower Japanese Government Bond yields, reducing the incentive for Japanese investors to switch from U.S. Treasuries to Japan's sovereign debt. Hayes also suggested that expanding the Federal Reserve's balance sheet by printing more money could complement this strategy. This intervention could potentially have positive implications for risk assets like Bitcoin. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.
